title: S-Tier Utilities Tool Manifest
description: Declares a tool to the platform kernel: what it provides, what it requires, how it loads, and what it is allowed to do. One manifest per tool, first-party or third-party. See docs/platform-observability-and-ecosystem.md.

id.description: Stable unique tool id (kebab-case). Used as the namespace for its capabilities and config dir.

apiVersion.description: Platform/kernel contract this tool was built against. The kernel refuses to load a tool whose apiVersion it does not support.

kind.description: How the kernel loads and isolates the tool. native = Rust module compiled in (full trust). mcp = out-of-process MCP server (sandboxed; recommended for third parties). webview = sandboxed iframe panel.

provides.description: Capabilities this tool offers to others. A capability is a named, versioned contract (interface), not an implementation.

requires.description: Capabilities this tool consumes. The kernel resolves these from the providers and hands the tool a handle to each (least privilege: a tool can only host.use() what it lists here).

permissions.description: Coarse capability-independent grants the user approves on install and the kernel enforces at runtime.

dashboards.description: Paths (relative to the tool bundle) to Grafana dashboard JSON files. The kernel provisions these into Grafana when the Observability Pack is installed.

entry.description: How to load the implementation. Shape depends on `kind`.

module.description: Rust module / registration symbol (informational for native; resolved at compile time).

command.description: Executable to launch for stdio transport.

page.description: Relative path to the panel HTML loaded in a sandboxed iframe.

capabilityName.description: Capability id without version, dotted namespace (e.g. 'bacnet.read', 'timeseries').

version.description: Exact version of the contract this tool implements (semver).
Semver range the provider must satisfy (e.g. '^1.0', '~1.2', '1.0').

optional.description: If true, the tool loads even when no provider exists and must degrade gracefully. Use this for timeseries and other ambient services.

permission.description: A coarse runtime grant enforced by the kernel's permission broker.

ui.tagline: Discover BACnet/IP devices, browse objects, read & write points.
Continuously log BACnet points to InfluxDB and chart them in Grafana.
